data.body:
OVERLAY CATALOG — LLM prompts / skills
- Source: Anthropic prompting + skill-authoring best practices (PROPRIETARY — paraphrase only) + OWASP LLM Top 10 / Prompt-Injection Cheat Sheet (CC-BY-SA — paraphrase to avoid share-alike) + permissive tool taxonomies: promptfoo (MIT) red-team plugins, garak (Apache) probes, DSPy signatures (MIT), Inspect AI (MIT).
- Dimensions (two halves): A) prompt/skill quality — clarity, structure/delimiting, role framing, examples/few-shot, output-format/schema, grounding, context-justification/conciseness, skill discovery metadata, appropriate degrees of freedom, consistency/durability, workflows/feedback, tool/function-def quality, eval-spec quality, calibrated emphasis (avoid over-prompting); B) safety/injection — instruction hierarchy, segregate untrusted content, least-privilege tool scoping, HITL for high-impact, system-prompt/secret hygiene, output validation, obfuscation resistance, jailbreak/override resistance, refusal/abstention, access-control awareness (RBAC/BOLA/BFLA), adversarial test coverage.
- Triggers: *.prompt/prompts/, SKILL.md/.claude/skills//.claude/agents//*.agent.md, .cursor/rules/CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/copilot-instructions, reviewers//system_prompt constants, tool-def schemas, eval specs (*.eval.*, promptfooconfig, Inspect tasks, DSPy signatures); content signals (SYSTEM_PROMPT constants, name+description frontmatter, function-schema markers).
- LICENSE FLAG: best sources proprietary (Anthropic/OpenAI) -> paraphrase; OWASP CC-BY-SA -> paraphrase; permissive tool taxonomies usable with notice. Author rubric in own words.
